title: PostgreSQL Metadata, Outbox Events, and Orphan Repair · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs postgresql metadata, outbox events, and orphan repair from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed metadata and object reconciliation model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript System Of Record Boundary · changed case · Review System Of Record B…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9635 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates System Of Record Boundary without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript System Of Record Boundary · changed case · Review System Of Record B….

stimulus.title: Orphan Repair case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1003 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Deleting every unreferenced key found in one list pass is safe garbage collection. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost. Artifact under review: typed metadata and object reconciliation model.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Orphan Repair without its stated preconditions, omit the typed metadata and object reconciliation model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Orphan Repair for typed metadata and object reconciliation model.
The record names the assumption, applies Orphan Repair, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Orphan Repair record says typed metadata and object reconciliation model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Orphan Repair.
